description: I was standing in the riparian area below the dam at Stalker Lake. Finally, I had a flock of birds at hand. A WAVI popped up into view, and then flew off. As I continued pishing, another vireo (coming from same general vicinity as WAVI) flew in and landed on same exposed branch. It was entirely rather bright yellow beneath. It was unmarked (no streaks, wingbars, etc) above and fairly bright green, excepting a medium gray crown and nape that contrasted with the back. The supercilium was white. A blackish line started just below eye and extended forward (thru eye, so to speak) to bill. There was only a hint of the vague but large white eye arcs seen on WAVIs. The bill appeared small for a WAVI. I watched the bird for a while as it fed, getting views in shade and sun, and the colors held true.
